dbaTerm: alternate name (entities); trade name (sole proprietors, county)
llcFilesWith: NJ Division of Revenue and Enterprise Services (Form C-150G, online or paper)
soleProprietorFilesWith: County clerk of the county where the business is located (trade name certificate)
feeBasis: The $50 is the Division's fee per alternate name registration for an entity, and a five-year renewal is another $50. County clerks set their own trade name certificate fees for sole proprietors and partnerships.
renewalRule: Renewable for further five-year periods. The renewal window opens three months before expiration and closes at the expiration date.
protectionNote: The state's guidance says registration legally links the alternate name to the entity so it can trade under it; N.J.S.A. 42:2C-9 frames the filing as a precondition for using the name, not a grant of exclusive rights in it.
searchNote: Registered alternate names live in Division records. The free public window is the Business Name Availability search, which returns a verdict and similar names; browsing the full records is New Jersey's paid Business Records Service.
notes: New Jersey splits by owner type the way Texas does, with its own vocabulary: the state's guidance is explicit that a domestic entity does not use a "dba" but registers an alternate name with the Division, while sole proprietors and partnerships record a trade name with their county clerk. An entity may not conduct business under an unregistered alternate name unless it also uses its actual name non-deceptively.

sources.citation: NJ Division of Revenue and Enterprise Services
sources.title: Registering an alternate name (Form C-150G, $50, five years, renewal window)
